title: World News: From Diplomacy to Tragedies
summary: Today we cover global events, including presidential activities, conflicts, and plane crash investigations.
script:
Hello everyone, and welcome to your daily news podcast. I'm your host, and we've got a lot to talk about today. We'll be looking at what's happening around the world, from big political moves to tragic events and ongoing conflicts. So, let's jump right in.

First up, let's talk about America. The country is in a time of war right now. But what's the president doing? Well, his work has been a mix of different things. It includes serious talks, which we call diplomacy, but also some lighter activities. And sometimes, his actions have been a bit strange, even surreal. It's a very interesting situation to watch as America deals with these challenges.

Now, we move over to Ukraine, where the situation remains very serious. Officials there have reported a massive wave of Russian attacks. These attacks have been very bad, among the worst seen in the last ten days. Sadly, at least five people have been killed in these strikes. The damage is spread across many parts of the country. Reports say that eleven different regions have seen damage. It's a truly tough time for the people in Ukraine, as these attacks continue to cause harm and destruction across their land. Our thoughts are with everyone affected by this ongoing conflict.

Next, we have some very sad news from LaGuardia airport. There was a terrible plane crash there. Two pilots lost their lives in this incident. Right now, authorities are looking into what caused the crash. A man named Leo Medina was on another plane nearby. He told the BBC what he saw. He said it looked 'like the plane got cut in half.' This is a very shocking description of the accident. It shows just how bad the crash was. An investigation is now fully underway to find out exactly what happened and why. We send our deepest sympathies to the families of the pilots.

Let's switch gears and go to Angola in Africa. There's a trial happening there right now. A group of people, both Russians and Angolans, are in court. They are accused of trying to start protests against the government. These protests are sometimes called anti-government protests. However, the people on trial say this is not true. They deny all the accusations. So, the court will have to decide what really happened. It's a case that highlights international connections and local politics. It will be interesting to see how this trial unfolds and what the outcome will be. This kind of situation shows the complex relationships between different countries and their people.

Finally, we have another tragic plane crash to report. This happened in Colombia. A Hercules military transport plane went down. This was a very large plane used by the military. It crashed only minutes after it had taken off. The crash happened near Colombia's southern border, close to Peru. Sadly, 66 people were killed in this accident. And even more heartbreaking, the search is still on for four missing soldiers. The authorities are working hard to find them. This is a devastating loss for the military and for Colombia. Our hearts go out to all the families who lost loved ones in this terrible crash. It's a stark reminder of the dangers faced by those in service.

And that brings us to the end of our news update for today. We covered some heavy topics, from global leadership during wartime to the ongoing struggles in Ukraine, and the tragic plane crashes in LaGuardia and Colombia, along with political tensions in Angola. We always aim to bring you the most important stories in a simple way. Thank you for tuning in and listening. We appreciate you being here. Make sure to join us again tomorrow for more news and updates. Until then, take care.
